laws.title: Public Law 26-186 | Secret Service Jurisdiction Reform Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Reorganizes Secret Service jurisdiction, closure authority, and related enforcement rules across sections 4-9; it is a permanent stand-alone reform without a numbered United States Code amendment.
laws.chronology: Source is current and operative; no later enactment in the reviewed materials repeals or displaces it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain was resolved; the act functions as a standalone permanent reform.
laws.status_basis: The source text is intact and operative. It regulates Secret Service jurisdiction and related closure procedures and contains no self-repeal or sunset.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML uses a statutory note at the Secret Service anchor, which is the right treatment type, but the prose reads as generic boilerplate and should be tightened rather than expanded into permanent Code text.

recommended_actions: Keep the act as a concise statutory note under the Secret Service/title 18 anchor.
Remove any generic boilerplate if it appears in the note prose.
Do not paste the full act into permanent Code text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Source is current and operative; no later enactment in the reviewed materials repeals or displaces it.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep the act as a concise statutory note under the Secret Service/title 18 anchor. | Remove any generic boilerplate if it appears in the note prose. | Do not paste the full act into permanent Code text.

laws.chronology: Expired appropriation. It does not control current Code text.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No Code transfer or redesignation issue; the act belongs, if retained, only as historical context.
laws.status_basis: The source is intact but expired by its own fiscal-year terms; no current Code text should flow from it.

current_implementation.detail: Any codification entry for this measure should remain historical-only and must not be surfaced as active law.

recommended_actions: Retain only a historical note if the repository keeps the act at all.
Do not treat the appropriation as active Code text.
Do not create or expand permanent United States Code language from this expired measure.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Expired appropriation. It does not control current Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 26-188 | Departmental Reports Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals 50 U.S.C. 3371a and establishes a departmental reporting regime tied to the covered executive-branch reporting duties.
laws.chronology: Current and operative; the act is not superseded in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal of 50 U.S.C. 3371a plus a reporting framework; no separate transfer chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: The source text is current and permanent. It directly affects 50 U.S.C. 3371a and establishes a continuing reporting regime.

current_implementation.detail: The existing implementation leaves 50 U.S.C. 3371a reserved, which matches the repeal component, and the repository plans already treat the law as incorporated. The entry should stay clean and not be rewritten as a generic note.

recommended_actions: Confirm the repeal marking at 50 U.S.C. 3371a and preserve the reserved section cleanly.
Retain the reporting provisions in the existing incorporated treatment.
Do not downgrade the act to a pure historical note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current and operative; the act is not superseded in the reviewed materials.
laws.final_recommended_action: Confirm the repeal marking at 50 U.S.C. 3371a and preserve the reserved section cleanly. | Retain the reporting provisions in the existing incorporated treatment. | Do not downgrade the act to a pure historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 26-190 | The Great Reform Initiative of the Judiciary
laws.final_statutory_text: Reworks Title 28 by amending chapter 1, chapter 13, chapter 15, chapter 43, chapter 5, and sections 136, 331, and 631-639, with cross-article judiciary reforms.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the reviewed plans say the Title 28 amendments are already incorporated in the existing sections.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct amendment package to existing Title 28 sections and chapters; no separate transfer chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: The source text is current and operative. It makes a broad package of direct Title 28 amendments and is already reflected in the existing Code structure.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch and plan materials already point to the Title 28 amendment locations, including sections 136, 331, and 631-639. No additional XML rewrite is needed for this law.

recommended_actions: Preserve the existing integrated Title 28 text.
Do not add a redundant note-only treatment.
Keep the chronology record but leave the incorporated amendments in place.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment; the reviewed plans say the Title 28 amendments are already incorporated in the existing sections.
laws.final_recommended_action: Preserve the existing integrated Title 28 text. | Do not add a redundant note-only treatment. | Keep the chronology record but leave the incorporated amendments in place.

laws.title: Public Law 26-191 | Federal Employment Reform Act of 2024 (FERA24)
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals and revises federal employment rules, including the provisions identified in sections 4-5 and 9-10; it is a free-standing permanent reform rather than a Code rewrite with one positive-law target.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No single positive-law target was resolved; the act reads as a free-standing reform with repeal and amendment effects.
laws.status_basis: The source text is current and operative. It is a free-standing federal employment reform measure with repeal/amendment effects, not a new positive-law Code section.

current_implementation.detail: The present entry treats the law as repealed or superseded, but the source is current. It should instead remain a current note-only record tied to the employment subject matter.

recommended_actions: Replace any repealed-or-superseded treatment with a current note-only entry.
Keep the act as a freestanding reform note rather than permanent Code text.
Do not infer a single positive-law section target where the source does not supply one.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative.
laws.final_recommended_action: Replace any repealed-or-superseded treatment with a current note-only entry. | Keep the act as a freestanding reform note rather than permanent Code text. | Do not infer a single positive-law section target where the source does not supply one.

laws.title: Public Law 27-192 | Presidential Libraries and Better Public Records Management Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Amends 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b), and revises the public-records and presidential-libraries framework tied to the enactment's remaining provisions.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the direct amendment to 18 U.S.C. 2071 remains operative.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct amendment to existing 18 U.S.C. text plus related record-management provisions; no transfer chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: The source is current and contains an express amendment to 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b) together with related records provisions.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML/origin-main state does not show the 18 U.S.C. 2071(a)-(b) amendment, so a note-only treatment is not enough. The amendment to the existing Title 18 text must be carried through.

recommended_actions: Apply the strike-and-insert amendments to 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b).
Keep the related public-records material only as provenance or note material.
Do not rely on a title 44 note alone for the operative Code change.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment; the direct amendment to 18 U.S.C. 2071 remains operative.
laws.final_recommended_action: Apply the strike-and-insert amendments to 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b). | Keep the related public-records material only as provenance or note material. | Do not rely on a title 44 note alone for the operative Code change.

laws.title: Public Law 27-194 | Fast Food Workers Day Act of 2024
laws.final_statutory_text: Adds National Fast Food Workers Day, June 7, to 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) after Memorial Day.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the holiday insertion remains operative.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct amendment to the federal holiday list in 5 U.S.C. 6103(a); no transfer chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: The source is current and operates by inserting a holiday into 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).

current_implementation.detail: The current XML already includes 'National Fast Food Workers Day, June 7.' after Memorial Day in 5 U.S.C. 6103(a), and the amendment note tracks Pub. L. 27-194.

recommended_actions: Retain the inserted holiday text in 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).
Do not convert the amendment into a mere note.
Preserve the existing provenance note if the repository uses one.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment; the holiday insertion remains operative.
laws.final_recommended_action: Retain the inserted holiday text in 5 U.S.C. 6103(a). | Do not convert the amendment into a mere note. | Preserve the existing provenance note if the repository uses one.

laws.title: Public Law 27-195 | The Preservation of Laws Act (PLA)
laws.final_statutory_text: A stand-alone preservation and codification-process statute that records rules for preserving laws and codification materials; no separate positive-law section amendment was identified.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative, but it does not require a new positive-law Code section.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain was resolved; the act is best treated as a note-only codification-process record.
laws.status_basis: The source is current and operative, but it speaks to preservation and codification procedure rather than requiring a new positive-law Code section.

current_implementation.detail: The act should remain a concise note tied to the codification-process/title 1 or title 44 anchor, and any boilerplate should be removed so the repository does not imply permanent Code text.

recommended_actions: Keep the law as a concise note-only record.
Move or confirm the anchor at the codification-process subject matter rather than permanent text.
Remove any boilerplate that obscures the preservation purpose of the act.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment; the source is intact and operative, but it does not require a new positive-law Code section.
